---
title: "Reproducibility"
---

The final part brings the workflow back into view. A defensible analysis is not
complete when the model has been fitted. It must still be documented, regenerated,
checked, and communicated in a way that leaves a visible path from source data to
reported conclusion.

This closing chapter therefore treats reproducibility as part of statistical
practice rather than as a separate technical concern. The same care applied earlier
to design, assumptions, and model choice must also be applied to how the analysis
is stored, rendered, and revised.

This is the point at which the whole course closes its loop: question, design,
data, model, interpretation, and transparent reporting all have to remain joined.
